rulings.Case Title: JOHN LENGYEL et al VS. NICHOLAS PREPOUSES et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Establishing Admissions
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday June 17, 2015, Line 1, DEFENDANT NICHOLAS PREPOUSES, MARY PREPOUSES' Motion For Order Establishing Admissions. Pro Tem Judge Peter Catalanotti, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Defendants Nicholas and Mary Prepouses' unopposed Motion For Order Establishing Admissions is GRANTED. Defendants shall prepare a proposed order in Word format and email to pcc@manningllp.com before 5pm today, copying all counsel or unrepresented parties. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to pcc@manningllp.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: LAURA ALBERT ET AL VS. INTERNATIONAL HIGH SCHOOL, ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, June 17, 2015, Line 10, DEFENDANT EMMANUEL AL THANI'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. Overruled in part and sustained in part. Sustained without leave to amend as to the seventh cause of action for conspiracy. Civil conspiracy is not a cause of action. (Applied Equipment Corp. v. Litton Saudi Arabia Ltd. (1994) 7 Cal.4th 503, 510-11.) However, Plaintiff may amend the complaint to add civil conspiracy allegations. Sustained with 10 days leave to amend on the uncertainty ground. Plaintiff is given leave to amend to clarify the use of "Al-Thani" throughout the complaint, as there are two defendants with that last name. Overruled as to the eighth cause of action for intentional infliction of emotional distress. The First Amended Complaint ("FAC") adequately pleads facts establishing extreme and outrageous conduct. (FAC, pars. 115-120.) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: LAURA ALBERT ET AL VS. INTERNATIONAL HIGH SCHOOL, ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, June 17, 2015, Line 10, DEFENDANTS INTERNATIONAL HIGH SCHOOL, AND JANE CAMBLIN'S MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. Granted with leave to amend in part and without leave to amend in part. The motion is granted with 10 days leave to amend as to the prayer for punitive damages, in light of the ruling of demurrer. Because Plaintiff has not adequately pled a cause of action for intentional infliction of emotional distress, the punitive damages claim fails. The remaining causes of action are based in negligence and contract and therefore do not give rise to a punitive damages claim. The motion is granted without leave to amend as to the reference to the California Constitution (paragraph 74) as irrelevant. The First Amended Complaint ("FAC") alleges that International High School ("IHS") is a private school. The motion is granted without leave to amend as to the references to IHS's "employees, agents, directors, teachers, staff, and/or administration." A cause of action cannot be asserted against a nonparty. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: LAURA ALBERT ET AL VS. INTERNATIONAL HIGH SCHOOL, ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, June 17, 2015, Line 10, DEFENDANTS INTERNATIONAL HIGH SCHOOL, AND JANE CAMBLIN'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. Overruled in part and sustained with 10 days leave to amend in part. The demurrer is overruled as to the causes of action for breach of contract and breach of the covenant of good faith and fair dealing as to Defendant International High School ("IHS") only. The First Amended Complaint ("FAC") adequately alleges that there was a valid contract between Plaintiff and IHS. Further, the relationship between a private school and a student can be contractual in nature. (Zumbrun v. University of Southern California (1972) 25 Cal.App.3d 1, 10, "The basic legal relation between a student and a private university or college is contractual in nature. The catalogues, bulletins, circulars, and regulations of the institution made available to the matriculant become a part of the contract.") The demurrer is sustained with 10 days leave to amend as to Defendant Jane Camblin. There are no allegations in the FAC establishing that Camblin was a party to the contract. Overruled as to the negligent supervision cause of action. The FAC adequately alleges facts establishing that it was foreseeable that Plaintiff would be injured by increased OCD and PTSD symptoms if the bullying at school persisted. The FAC also adequately alleges that Defendants did not reasonably respond to the bullying complaints. Sustained with 10 days leave to amend as to the intentional infliction of emotional distress cause of action. The FAC does not plead facts establishing that Defendants engaged in extreme or outrageous conduct. Sustained with 10 days leave to amend as to the negligent infliction of emotional distress cause of action. Plaintiff is granted leave to amend to plead facts establishing that Defendants had a special duty to avoid negligently causing emotional distress. Plaintiff cites no cases that hold that the special duty arises merely because of a school-student relationship, and therefore, more facts are necessary to establish that there is a duty. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Plaintiff David Bryant's Motion To Deem Matters Admitted Against Let It Flho Lessee, Inc. And For Sanctions is GRANTED. The request for Sanctions is DENIED. CCP § 2023.040. The Court will sign the proposed order. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to pcc@manningllp.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: STEVEN NICHOLS VS. WILLIAM HO ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaintiff'S Notice Of Motion And Motion For A Protective Order And Motion To Quash As To Deft'S Subpoena To Education Management, Llc
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Wednesday June 17, 2015, Line 7, PLAINTIFF STEVEN NICHOLS' Motion For A Protective Order And Motion To Quash As To Defendant's Subpoena To Education Management, LLC. Pro Tem Judge Peter Catalanotti, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Plaintiff's Motion to Quash Subpoena is GRANTED and DENIED, as follows. The subpoena is limited to the following employment records: all documents that refer or relate to any head injury/brain injury, memory/cognitive issues, shoulder injury, back injury, and neck injury; regular earnings and bonus earnings records; attendance records; and health benefits records. The time limit for the subpoena shall be within the past 7 years. The production shall not include any tax records of plaintiff, including W2's or 1099's. All requests for sanctions are DENIED. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to pcc@manningllp.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)
